heading: Omitted
label: 505 to 518 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections 505 to 518, relating to Territory of Hawaii, were omitted in view of admission of Hawaii into the Union.Section 505, , , prescribed the style of process in courts.act Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 1131 Stat. 144Section 506, , §§ 95, 96, , made certain fisheries free to United States citizens subject to vested rights.act Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 33931 Stat. 160Section 507, , , provided for condemnation of private fishing rights.act Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 9631 Stat. 160Section 508, acts , ; , title IX, § 913, formerly title VI, § 611, , provided that jurisdiction of health laws remain under the control of Territory of Hawaii.Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 9731 Stat. 160July 1, 1944, ch. 37358 Stat. 714Section 509, , , allowed American registry of Hawaiian-registered vessels.act Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 9831 Stat. 161Section 510, acts , ; , §§ 1, 20, , 561; , , placed control of wharves and landings under Territory of Hawaii.Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 8931 Stat. 159Aug. 4, 1949, ch. 39363 Stat. 496June 29, 1954, ch. 41868 Stat. 323Section 511, acts , ; , ; , ; , , , gave to Territory of Hawaii control of public property ceded to United States by Republic of Hawaii and allowed transfer of title to political subdivisions of Territory.Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 9131 Stat. 159May 27, 1910, ch. 258, § 736 Stat. 447June 19, 1930, ch. 54646 Stat. 789Pub. L. 85–719, § 172 Stat. 709Aug. 21, 1958Section 512, , , made provision for sale, lease, or disposal of personal or movable property ceded to the United States.act May 26, 1906, ch. 256134 Stat. 204Section 513, , §§ 1, 2, , called for recoinage of Hawaiian silver coins into subsidiary silver coins of the United States.act Jan. 14, 1903, ch. 18632 Stat. 770Section 514, , , allowed any collector of customs or internal revenue to exchange United States coins in his custody for Hawaiian coins under regulations of Secretary of the Treasury.act Jan. 14, 1903, ch. 186, § 332 Stat. 771Section 515, , , allowed recoinage of mutilated or abraded Hawaiian coins into subsidiary coinage of the United States by any mint of the United States.act Jan. 14, 1903, ch. 186, § 432 Stat. 771Section 516, , , made unlawful circulation as money of any silver certificate issued by government of Hawaiian Islands prior to .act Jan. 14, 1903, ch. 186, § 632 Stat. 771Jan. 14, 1903Section 517, , , limited redemption of Hawaiian silver certificates or silver coin to redemption in manner and upon conditions set for recoinage of Hawaiian silver.act Jan. 14, 1903, ch. 186, § 732 Stat. 771Section 518, , as added , , prohibited employment as a mechanic or laborer on any public work of persons not citizens of the United States or eligible for such citizenship.act Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 105July 9, 1921, ch. 42, § 31542 Stat. 120
